title: Silent Payment Failure Cascade — Stripe TLS Cert Rotation
correct_root_cause: Stripe API rotated their TLS certificate chain at 09:12 UTC. Our Python Stripe SDK (v4.2.0) uses a bundled CA certificate store that did not include the new intermediate CA. The SDK connection pool (size: 10) exhausted itself retrying failed handshakes. Each failed connection held a pool slot for 30s before timeout, leaving new payment requests queued indefinitely and ultimately failing silently with a generic 'connection pool exhausted' exception that was swallowed by a bare except clause.
detection_gap: The 0.3% initial failure rate was below all automated alert thresholds (minimum 1% for P2, 5% for P1). The failure was first detected by a customer complaint in #support-escalations at 09:58 — 43 minutes after onset. No synthetic canary tests cover the payment checkout flow end-to-end. The bare except in payment_processor.py masked the error as a generic internal server error.
contributing_factors: Stripe SDK bundled CA store not automatically updated — pinned to SDK version
Bare except clause in payment_processor.py swallowed connection pool errors
No end-to-end synthetic monitoring for payment checkout flow
Alert threshold too high: 1% failure rate floor missed 0.3% initial signal
Connection pool size (10) too small for retry-storm under certificate failures

slack.message: Getting DMs from 3 enterprise customers saying checkout is broken. No error shown to user, cart just hangs.
Checking payment service. Error rate is 1.2% — just crossed our alert threshold. Investigating.
Database looks healthy, no slow queries. Payment service CPU/memory normal. This is weird.
Found it — connection pool exhausted errors in the payment logs. But why? We haven't changed anything.
Stripe status page shows no incidents. But I can reproduce the TLS handshake failure from our bastion host with openssl s_client against api.stripe.com
pip install stripe --upgrade fixed it. The SDK was bundling an old CA cert. Deploying now.

logs.message: stripe.error.APIConnectionError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='api.stripe.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /v1/payment_intents
ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: unable to get local issuer certificate (_ssl.c:1123)
Connection pool queue depth: 847 pending requests. Pool size: 10/10 occupied.

github.author: dependabot
github.message: chore: bump stripe from 4.2.0 to 7.1.0 [NOT MERGED]

github.author: jsmith
github.message: feat: add idempotency keys to payment retry logic

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Internal database slowdown causing payment timeouts and cascade failures
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: Database metrics show zero slow queries. All payment errors contain 'api.stripe.com' in the stack trace — the failure is at the external Stripe API call, not the internal DB. Latency spike to 30s matches the connection pool timeout (30s), not DB response times.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Recent code deployment to payment-service introduced a regression
expected_hypothesis_sequence.rejection_evidence: Last merged deployment was April 6 (idempotency key feature). Failures began April 8 at 09:15 — 2 days after deployment. The Dependabot PR to upgrade Stripe SDK was NOT merged. Deployment timeline does not align with failure onset.

expected_hypothesis_sequence.hypothesis: Stripe API TLS certificate rotation caused SDK connection pool exhaustion via SSL handshake failures
expected_hypothesis_sequence.confirming_evidence: Logs show ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: unable to get local issuer certificate starting at 09:16 — 1 minute after incident onset. Connection pool immediately saturated to 10/10 and stayed there for 2 hours. Each failed TLS handshake held a pool slot for 30s. The Dependabot PR to upgrade Stripe SDK (which bundles updated CA certs) was open but unmerged. Upgrading stripe package resolved the TLS verification issue at 11:22.

action_items: Upgrade stripe SDK to 7.x and add automated dependency update policy for security-sensitive packages
Remove bare except clauses in payment_processor.py — catch specific exception types and log with full stack trace
Add end-to-end synthetic canary: POST /checkout every 60s with a test card, alert on failure
Lower payment error rate alert threshold: P3 at 0.1%, P2 at 0.5%, P1 at 2%
Increase Stripe connection pool size to 50 and add pool utilization metric to dashboard
Add openssl s_client check to pre-deploy smoke test for all third-party TLS dependencies
Subscribe to Stripe changelog RSS for certificate rotation announcements
